bid_claims.quote_text: Each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A indicated that they were considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share, in cash.
Each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A indicated that they were considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share, in cash.
indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E)
indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E)
Hudson’s Bay’s proposal included a price of $15.25 per share of common stock
The joint proposal from Sponsor E and Sponsor G included an indicative price range of $14.50–$15.50 per share of common stock

bid_claims.bid_value_unit: per share of common stock in cash
bid_claims.quote_text: the $16 per share of common stock in cash to be paid to Saks’ shareholders in the proposed merger

event_claims.description: Sponsor A made an unsolicited initial contact expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: In February 2013, Stephen I. Sadove, Saks’ Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, received an unsolicited phone call from a representative of a private equity firm, which we refer to as Sponsor A, expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Mr. Sadove met with Richard Baker of Hudson’s Bay and discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 1, 2013, Mr. Sadove met with Richard Baker, the Director, Governor, and Chief Executive Officer of Hudson’s Bay, at the request of Mr. Baker and discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay.

event_claims.description: Sponsor A and Sponsor E considered participating in a potential joint acquisition of Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 26, 2013, Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with each of Sponsor A and Sponsor E, who were considering participating in a potential joint acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Saks and Hudson’s Bay entered into a confidentiality agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 30, 2013, Saks and Hudson’s Bay entered into a confidentiality agreement.

event_claims.description: The board authorized implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with potential acquirors could be reached.
event_claims.quote_text: authorized (1) the implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with one of the potential acquirors which had executed a confidentiality agreement could be reached on terms that would offer attractive value to Saks shareholders

event_claims.description: Hudson’s Bay was preliminarily prepared to submit a proposal to acquire Saks at an indicative price range of $15 to $15.25 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).

event_claims.description: Sponsor A and Sponsor E were preliminarily prepared to submit a proposal to acquire Saks at an indicative price range of $15 to $16 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).

event_claims.description: Goldman Sachs distributed a draft merger agreement and process details to Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E, requesting offers by July 11, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 2, 2013, at the direction of Saks, Goldman Sachs distributed a draft merger agreement and process details to each of Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E, with a request for submission of offers for an all-cash acquisition of Saks, along with comments on the draft merger agreement, no later than July 11, 2013.

event_claims.description: Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor G.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 8, 2013, Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor G.

event_claims.description: Hudson’s Bay submitted a proposal expressing continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Sponsor E together with Sponsor G submitted a proposal expressing continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Saks was informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process.
event_claims.quote_text: Saks was subsequently informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process

event_claims.description: Sponsor A again joined Sponsor E's proposal as a primary participant.
event_claims.quote_text: Sponsor E would again be joined in its proposal by Sponsor A as a primary participant

event_claims.description: Saks received a letter from Company H purporting to propose to acquire Saks for $2.6 billion in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 21, 2013, Saks received a letter from Company H, a privately held company based in the U.S. unknown to Saks and its advisors, purporting to propose to acquire Saks for an aggregate price of $2.6 billion in cash, with no details or further information.

event_claims.description: Company H did not have further contact after Goldman Sachs attempted to follow up on the purported offer.
event_claims.quote_text: Neither Saks nor Goldman Sachs received any subsequent communications from Company H.

event_claims.description: Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that it was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 24, 2013, representatives of Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, Hudson’s Bay was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock

event_claims.description: The board authorized management and advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson’s Bay to determine if an agreement could be reached.
event_claims.quote_text: The board authorized senior management and Saks’ advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson’s Bay to determine if an agreement could be reached.

event_claims.description: Mr. Sadove and Mr. Baker discussed Hudson’s Bay's willingness to proceed at $16 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 25, 2013, Mr. Sadove and Mr. Baker briefly spoke telephonically regarding Hudson’s Bay’s willingness to proceed with the potential acquisition of Saks at a price of $16 per share of common stock

event_claims.description: The board approved the merger and merger agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: Following extensive discussion, the board unanimously agreed to approve the merger and the merger agreement, and the transactions contemplated thereby, substantially in the form presented to the board, and to recommend the approval of the merger agreement by Saks’ shareholders.

event_claims.actor_label: Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub
event_claims.description: Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents.
event_claims.quote_text: Following the board’s approval of the merger and the merger agreement, Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents later on July 28, 2013.

participation_count_claims.quote_text: Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).
On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
